description: The buttons on the side should be fully liquid glass, have no actual background of themselves. And then the play pause button doesn't need the glass background. It should just be the play pause button with maybe 0.5 opacity.

results.identified_issue: Two targets: the side buttons are the feedActionRail in DiscoverView (railButton/railGlassBackground) and the play/pause 'button' is the centre tap flash in NowPlayingContent (flashView), since on-screen transport buttons were replaced by tap gestures. For the rail I removed the tint entirely rather than just off the primary — the request said the buttons should have 'no actual background of themselves,' and the only button with a fill was the tinted primary; all three are now plain .regular.interactive() glass. For the flash I removed the .ultraThinMaterial circle and set the glyph to 0.5 opacity per the request. If 'play pause button' turns out to mean a persistent control I haven't found, this is the wrong target — but there is no persistent transport in the feed, only the tap flash.
results.detailed_description: Two changes in the Discover feed. First, the side action-rail buttons (Answer / Browse / show-hide question): the primary 'Answer' button used to sit on tinted Liquid Glass, which read as a solid accent-colored disc. I removed the tint so all three buttons are now plain regular interactive Liquid Glass with no fill of their own — the button is the glass floating over the video, not a colored chip. Second, the play/pause indicator that flashes in the center on tap: it previously sat on an ultraThinMaterial circle. I dropped that glass disc so it's now just the bare play/pause glyph at 0.5 opacity, fading on its own after a beat. I couldn't run the app to screenshot it (builds happen downstream), so the visual is verified by reading the SwiftUI, not by capture — worth a look on-device to confirm the clear glass reads well over a bright video frame.
results.summary: Made the side rail buttons clear Liquid Glass and stripped the glass disc off the play/pause flash
